perturbation.original_text: Ross & Moncure recognizes that the duties of secrecy and non -disclosure continue to apply indefinitely even after all service engagements have terminated. Ross & Moncure undertakes to take and maintain the technical and organizational steps required to guarantee data security and prevent their alteration, loss and unauthorized processing or access, according to the security level required pursuant to the aforementioned legislation.
perturbation.changed_text: Ross & Moncure recognizes that the duties of secrecy and non -disclosure continue to apply indefinitely even after all service engagements have terminated. Ross & Moncure undertakes to take and maintain the technical and organizational steps required to guarantee data security and prevent their alteration, loss and unauthorized processing or access.
perturbation.explanation: By removing the phrase "according to the security level required pursuant to the aforementioned legislation", the obligation to maintain data security is weakened and made ambiguous. While Ross & Moncure still undertakes to maintain data security, the standard to which they must adhere is no longer clearly defined. This omission creates an in-text contradiction if a data breach occurs, and the client seeks to enforce a specific legislated security standard, as the contract no longer explicitly mandates compliance with such legislation, even though it mentions data security.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Obligations Regarding Confidential Information. Ross & Moncure pledges not to disclose any information to which it has access in the process of the performance of a Client engagement. Ross & Moncure also agrees to only request of the Client information necessary to carry out services requested by the Client. Ross & Moncure shall process information and data provided in accordance with the instructions given by the Client and shall refr ain from recording, reproducing, or storing data for superfluous reasons. This prohibition affects both hard -copy data and data on any electronic, magn etic, analogue or digital medium . Under no circumstances may it disclose such data to third parties witho ut written authorization from the Client, even merely for safekeeping. Ross & Moncure recognizes that the duties of secrecy and non -disclosure continue to apply indefinitely even after all service engagements have terminated. Ross & Moncure undertakes to take and maintain the technical and organizational steps required to guarantee data security and prevent their alteration, loss and unauthorized processing or access. T hese steps refer to the files, processing centers, premises, equipment, systems, programs and people involved in processing. Once the contractual service has been performed, Ross & Moncure agrees to hand back to the Client all of the raw data used in tax planning upon the Client’s written request. Ross & 726 North Washington Street | Alexandria, VA 22314 | Fax (703) 549- 6517 | Phone (703) 549- 5276 | www.rossmoncure.com | tax@rossmoncure.com Moncure does not agree to hand back and destroy copies of completed tax returns, as the American Institute of Certified Public Accountants and the Association for Accounting Administration both dictate th at best practice is to keep this work on hand .
perturbation.justification: The original text specifies that data security measures should be maintained according to the security level required by the aforementioned legislation. The changed text removes this specification, weakening the obligation to maintain data security to a specific legislated standard. The contradicted text references and depends on the security measures in the changed text for data handling, processing, and safekeeping. Removing the phrase from the Changed text creates a contradiction because there is no longer a specific standard of data security the company must adhere to, leading to potential disputes over whether the company met its obligations related to data security as referenced in the contradicted section.

perturbation.original_text: Once the contractual service has been performed, Ross & Moncure agrees to hand back to the Client all of the raw data used in tax planning upon the Client’s written request. Ross & 726 North Washington Street | Alexandria, VA 22314 | Fax (703) 549- 6517 | Phone (703) 549- 5276 | www.rossmoncure.com | tax@rossmoncure.com Moncure does not agree to hand back and destroy copies of completed tax returns, as the American Institute of Certified Public Accountants and the Association for Accounting Administration both dictate th at best practice is to keep this work on hand .
perturbation.changed_text: Once the contractual service has been performed, Ross & Moncure agrees to hand back to the Client all of the raw data used in tax planning upon the Client’s written request.
perturbation.explanation: The omission of the sentence "Ross & Moncure does not agree to hand back and destroy copies of completed tax returns, as the American Institute of Certified Public Accountants and the Association for Accounting Administration both dictate that best practice is to keep this work on hand" creates uncertainty regarding the disposition of completed tax returns. The client could reasonably assume, based on the remaining text, that all data, including completed returns, will be returned, leading to conflict. The contradiction arises because the contract implies returning all raw data, but the accepted practice is not to return finished tax returns due to regulatory reasons, making it unclear whether the 'completed tax returns' have to be returned as part of the raw data.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Obligations Regarding Confidential Information. Ross & Moncure pledges not to disclose any information to which it has access in the process of the performance of a Client engagement. Ross & Moncure also agrees to only request of the Client information necessary to carry out services requested by the Client. Ross & Moncure shall process information and data provided in accordance with the instructions given by the Client and shall refr ain from recording, reproducing, or storing data for superfluous reasons. This prohibition affects both hard -copy data and data on any electronic, magn etic, analogue or digital medium . Under no circumstances may it disclose such data to third parties witho ut written authorization from the Client, even merely for safekeeping. Ross & Moncure recognizes that the duties of secrecy and non -disclosure continue to apply indefinitely even after all service engagements have terminated. Ross & Moncure undertakes to take and maintain the technical and organizational steps required to guarantee data security and prevent their alteration, loss and unauthorized processing or access. T hese steps refer to the files, processing centers, premises, equipment, systems, programs and people involved in processing. Once the contractual service has been performed, Ross & Moncure agrees to hand back to the Client all of the raw data used in tax planning upon the Client’s written request.
perturbation.justification: The original text explicitly stated that Ross & Moncure does not agree to return completed tax returns, which aligns with the obligation of maintaining data security and confidentiality as stated in the contradicted text. The changed text omits this statement, implying that all raw data, including completed tax returns, will be returned, potentially contradicting the 'Obligations Regarding Confidential Information' which requires data security and prevents unauthorized processing or access. The omission creates a strong in-text contradiction because the changed text creates an ambiguity about whether completed tax returns have to be returned, violating client confidentiality. Therefore, the change directly breaks compatibility with the contradicted section. The reasonable reader would immediately recognize the potential unenforceability if completed returns were returned.

perturbation.original_text: Ross & Moncure shall bear the burden of any damages arising from negligence and/or lack of confidentiality, improper use, processing or communication of personal data, or any other infringeme nt of data protection rules. The full extent of these damages shall be determined by a court of competent jurisdiction.
perturbation.changed_text: Ross & Moncure shall bear the burden of any damages arising from negligence and/or lack of confidentiality, improper use, processing or communication of personal data, or any other infringeme nt of data protection rules.
perturbation.explanation: By removing "The full extent of these damages shall be determined by a court of competent jurisdiction.", the contract language becomes ambiguous. While Ross & Moncure is responsible for damages, the removal of how those damages are determined creates uncertainty and can be considered a contradiction. This is because earlier in the section, they promise to maintain the highest levels of security but now it is not explicit how the extent of a data breach will be measured. This also makes it more difficult to enforce in court as the burden of proof may be heavier now, given the lack of the defining sentance.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Rights and Remedies Ross & Moncure shall notify the undersigned Client immediately upon discovery of any unauthorized use or disclosure of Confidential Information or any other breach of this Agreement by an employee of Ross & Moncure , and will cooperate with the Disclosing Party in every reasonable way to help the Disclosing Party to regain possession of the Confidential Information and prevent its further unauthorized use or disclosure. Ross & Moncure shall bear the burden of any damages arising from negligence and/or lack of confidentiality, improper use, processing or communication of personal data, or any other infringeme nt of data protection rules.
perturbation.justification: The original text explicitly stated that a court of competent jurisdiction would determine the full extent of damages. The changed text removes this, creating ambiguity regarding how damages will be assessed, directly conflicting with the original intent and potentially creating issues with enforcement, given the lack of a defining sentence that was present in the original text. The contradicted text includes the sentence that was altered, so by removing it, a contradiction exists.

perturbation.original_text: Definit ion of Confidential Information. “Confidential Information” means non -public information that a party to this Agreement (“Disclosing Party ”) designates as being confidential to the party that receives such information (“Receiving Party”) or which, under the circumstances surrounding disclosure ought to be treated as confidential by the Receiving Party. Ross & Moncure recognizes Confidential Information to be any information or personal data that it receives from the Client in the process of tax preparation or planning .
perturbation.changed_text: Definit ion of Confidential Information. “Confidential Information” means non -public information that a party to this Agreement (“Disclosing Party ”) designates as being confidential to the party that receives such information (“Receiving Party”) or which, under the circumstances surrounding disclosure ought to be treated as confidential by the Receiving Party.
perturbation.explanation: By removing the sentence "Ross & Moncure recognizes Confidential Information to be any information or personal data that it receives from the Client in the process of tax preparation or planning", the contract becomes less specific about what constitutes confidential information. The earlier part of the definition depends on one party designating it as confidential. This omission introduces an in-text contradiction because the agreement becomes vague on what specific client information during tax preparation is always considered confidential, creating ambiguity and potentially limiting the scope of protection, as the assumption will be that only designated data has to be protected.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Obligations Regarding Confidential Information. Ross & Moncure pledges not to disclose any information to which it has access in the process of the performance of a Client engagement. Ross & Moncure also agrees to only request of the Client information necessary to carry out services requested by the Client. Ross & Moncure shall process information and data provided in accordance with the instructions given by the Client and shall refr ain from recording, reproducing, or storing data for superfluous reasons. This prohibition affects both hard -copy data and data on any electronic, magn etic, analogue or digital medium . Under no circumstances may it disclose such data to third parties witho ut written authorization from the Client, even merely for safekeeping. Ross & Moncure recognizes that the duties of secrecy and non -disclosure continue to apply indefinitely even after all service engagements have terminated. Ross & Moncure undertakes to take and maintain the technical and organizational steps required to guarantee data security and prevent their alteration, loss and unauthorized processing or access. T hese steps refer to the files, processing centers, premises, equipment, systems, programs and people involved in processing. Once the contractual service has been performed, Ross & Moncure agrees to hand back to the Client all of the raw data used in tax planning upon the Client’s written request. Ross & 726 North Washington Street | Alexandria, VA 22314 | Fax (703) 549- 6517 | Phone (703) 549- 5276 | www.rossmoncure.com | tax@rossmoncure.com Moncure does not agree to hand back and destroy copies of completed tax returns, as the American Institute of Certified Public Accountants and the Association for Accounting Administration both dictate th at best practice is to keep this work on hand .
perturbation.justification: The original text explicitly stated that Ross & Moncure recognized client information during tax preparation as confidential. The changed text omits this specific recognition, making the definition of 'Confidential Information' more general and dependent on designation by the disclosing party. This directly contradicts the 'Obligations Regarding Confidential Information' section, which details Ross & Moncure's duties of non-disclosure and data security without specifying that such information needs to be designated first. The omission creates ambiguity about what client information during tax preparation is automatically considered confidential, thus establishing an in-text contradiction as the obligations section assumes client data from tax preperation is automatically private while the changed definition relies on designation.

perturbation.original_text: Ross & Moncure may not subcontract the provision of any services agreed herein in whole or in part.
perturbation.explanation: Removing the clause "Ross & Moncure may not subcontract the provision of any services agreed herein in whole or in part." introduces ambiguity about whether subcontracting is allowed. Without this clause, there's no explicit prohibition against subcontracting, yet the agreement is silent on the conditions or responsibilities if subcontracting *does* occur. The contradiction arises if, without informing the Client or gaining express consent, Ross & Moncure subcontracts services, which conflicts with the implicit assumption of the client that Ross & Moncure would perform the service themselves.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Obligations Regarding Confidential Information. Ross & Moncure pledges not to disclose any information to which it has access in the process of the performance of a Client engagement. Ross & Moncure also agrees to only request of the Client information necessary to carry out services requested by the Client. Ross & Moncure shall process information and data provided in accordance with the instructions given by the Client and shall refr ain from recording, reproducing, or storing data for superfluous reasons. This prohibition affects both hard -copy data and data on any electronic, magn etic, analogue or digital medium . Ross & Moncure recognizes that the duties of secrecy and non -disclosure continue to apply indefinitely even after all service engagements have terminated. Ross & Moncure undertakes to take and maintain the technical and organizational steps required to guarantee data security and prevent their alteration, loss and unauthorized processing or access. T hese steps refer to the files, processing centers, premises, equipment, systems, programs and people involved in processing. Once the contractual service has been performed, Ross & Moncure agrees to hand back to the Client all of the raw data used in tax planning upon the Client’s written request. Ross & 726 North Washington Street | Alexandria, VA 22314 | Fax (703) 549- 6517 | Phone (703) 549- 5276 | www.rossmoncure.com | tax@rossmoncure.com Moncure does not agree to hand back and destroy copies of completed tax returns, as the American Institute of Certified Public Accountants and the Association for Accounting Administration both dictate th at best practice is to keep this work on hand .
perturbation.justification: The original text explicitly prohibited Ross & Moncure from subcontracting. The changed text, by omitting this prohibition, introduces ambiguity. The contradicted text outlines Ross & Moncure's obligations regarding confidential information, implying that Ross & Moncure will be directly responsible for handling client data. If Ross & Moncure subcontracts this service without explicit client consent, it directly contradicts the implicit assumption within the 'Obligations Regarding Confidential Information' section, as the client's data would then be handled by an unknown third party without necessarily being subject to the same strictures. This aligns with the 'Omissions - In Text Contradiction' definition because essential language was removed, creating a contradiction with obligations stated elsewhere. Therefore, a strong in-text contradiction exists.

perturbation.original_text: Ross & Moncure also agrees to only request of the Client information necessary to carry out services requested by the Client. Ross & Moncure shall process information and data provided in accordance with the instructions given by the Client and shall refr ain from recording, reproducing, or storing data for superfluous reasons.
perturbation.changed_text: Ross & Moncure also agrees to only request of the Client information necessary to carry out services requested by the Client. Ross & Moncure shall process information and data provided in accordance with the instructions given by the Client.
perturbation.explanation: By removing "and shall refrain from recording, reproducing, or storing data for superfluous reasons", it creates uncertainty. While they still are processing information in accordance with the Client's instructions, there's no longer a direct restriction on recording, reproducing, or storing data for 'superfluous reasons'. Thus it could be considered an in-text contradiction with the pledge to non-disclosure. This implies that the company can do whatever it wants now with the data.

Once the contractual service has been performed, Ross & Moncure agrees to hand back to the Client all of the raw data used in tax planning upon the Client’s written request. Ross & 726 North Washington Street | Alexandria, VA 22314 | Fax (703) 549- 6517 | Phone (703) 549- 5276 | www.rossmoncure.com | tax@rossmoncure.com Moncure does not agree to hand back and destroy copies of completed tax returns, as the American Institute of Certified Public Accountants and the Association for Accounting Administration both dictate th at best practice is to keep this work on hand .
perturbation.justification: The original text explicitly stated that Ross & Moncure would refrain from recording, reproducing, or storing data for superfluous reasons. The changed text removes this restriction, potentially allowing them to record, reproduce, or store data for any reason, even superfluous ones. This contradicts the section that pledges not to disclose any information to which it has access, as recording, reproducing, or storing data for superfluous reasons could lead to potential disclosures or misuse of confidential information, creating a direct conflict.
